title: Whose Inventory Is It? Transit & Consignment
event: Hollis Co.'s physical count totals $200,000, which includes $18,000 of goods held on consignment FROM another company. Not counted: a $15,000 purchase in transit shipped FOB shipping point, and $22,000 of Hollis goods out on consignment at a dealer. Compute correct ending inventory.

computationPaths.narration: Ownership, not location, decides inventory. FOB shipping point transfers title at the dock; consigned goods belong to the consignor wherever they sit.

steps.label: Add FOB shipping point purchase in transit

memorize.body: FOB shipping point → buyer owns in transit. FOB destination → seller owns until arrival. Consigned goods → always the CONSIGNOR's.
Work every item with one question: who holds TITLE at midnight on the balance sheet date?

questions.prompt: Count $200,000; consigned-in $18,000 included; FOB shipping point purchase in transit $15,000; goods out on consignment $22,000. Correct inventory = ?

distractors.feedback: That's the raw count — location, not ownership. Three title adjustments move it to 219,000.
You added the transit and consigned-out goods but forgot to REMOVE the 18,000 held for others: 219,000.
